behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration: failure can occur at the transcript schema handoff (speaker-tag fields dropped/renamed between STT client and summarizer input). Integration -> System: real STT API response structure/diarization metadata must be exercised; mocks can mask provider-specific edge cases. System -> Agentic: correctness depends on real diarization quality from the speech model, which can vary by audio and run conditions. Rejected Workflow: behavior is satisfied by validating one real STT attribution step; it does not require proving a multi-agentic sequence end-to-end.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real meeting audio sample through the real STT provider and the transcript-ingest boundary, then assert speaker labels are present, mapped to attendees, and preserved in the data passed to summarization.

behaviors.description: The structured summary contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration: failure appears at transcript-to-summary contract boundaries (assigned-task evidence may not be represented correctly in summarizer input/output schema). Integration -> System: real LLM API invocation and formatting constraints must be exercised; stubs can hide truncation/token-limit effects. System -> Agentic: pass/fail depends on model extraction quality (did it correctly identify each attendee-task assignment), which deterministic mocks cannot validate. Rejected Workflow: this behavior can be validated in a single summarization step from a prepared transcript; no required multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a transcript with known attendee task assignments to the real summarization model and assert each assigned attendee has at least one action item in structured output.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the "decisions" section of the summary, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration: failure can occur at structured-output assembly boundaries (decision candidates extracted but not placed into the dedicated section). Integration -> System: real model/API response shape and length effects must be tested; mocks can over-idealize section fidelity. System -> Agentic: deciding what is a 'key decision' and placing it correctly depends on real model judgment/output quality. Rejected Workflow: a single real summarization call over a transcript is sufficient to test section placement for decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a transcript containing explicit decisions to the real summarizer and assert those decisions are present in the structured `decisions` field, not only in free-form narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration: failure exists at splitter/orchestrator/merger handoffs (segment boundaries, ordering metadata, merge state). Integration -> System: real long-audio processing with external API calls is needed to expose timing, payload, and sequencing behavior not seen in pure in-process fakes. System -> Agentic: final coherence and cross-segment fidelity depend on real model outputs per segment and merge summarization quality. Agentic -> Workflow: behavior explicitly requires multiple agentic steps in sequence (segment-by-segment transcription/summarization then merged output).
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through the production segmentation pipeline, verify sequential segment processing order, then validate merged summary coherence and completeness across segment boundaries.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output — completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration: failure emerges across multiple module handoffs (upload service, job orchestration, transcript store, summarizer, output delivery). Integration -> System: requires real I/O and external API interactions across the pipeline; in-process doubles can miss orchestration and operational failures. System -> Agentic: correctness includes successful real model transcription/summarization behavior, not just transport success. Agentic -> Workflow: this is explicitly a multi-step agentic pipeline (STT then LLM summarization) executed end-to-end without human intervention.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run on a real meeting recording through production-like interfaces and assert successful autonomous completion with valid structured output delivered to the target sink.
